title: Prevent duplicate/repeated slug intents from bypassing gate protections in the same bridge loop
subsystem: bridge entry gating / duplicate intent handling

context.problem_statement: Prior approved audits found that duplicate or repeated intents for the same slug could bypass gate protections within the same bridge cycle. With the two risk.py fixes now staged, the next critical task is to make sure repeated slug intents cannot reopen or bypass guard logic inside a single bridge loop.
context.manager_intent: Apply the smallest safe patch so a slug rejected once in a bridge iteration cannot still open through a repeated intent in that same iteration.

objective: Eliminate same-loop duplicate/repeated slug bypass behavior in the bridge entry flow while preserving normal valid trade flow.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\model_bridge.py only if strictly required to remove duplicate slug emission at the source and the worker documents why bot_core-only protection is insufficient
forbidden_actions: Read every file in the repo
required_work: Inspect the live bridge loop in bot_core.py where intents are iterated and open_position() can be reached
Identify the smallest safe place to track and block duplicate/repeated slug intents within a single bridge iteration
Ensure that once a slug is rejected or otherwise consumed in a given loop iteration, a repeated instance cannot still reach open_position() in that same iteration
Preserve valid normal entry flow for distinct slugs and valid >= threshold signals
Keep logging style consistent and minimal
acceptance_criteria: Repeated intents for the same slug cannot bypass gate protections in the same bridge loop
A slug rejected once in an iteration cannot still open later in that same iteration
Distinct valid slugs still proceed normally
No files outside allowed scope are modified unless explicitly documented under conditional scope
If model_bridge.py is modified, python -m py_compile core/model_bridge.py passes
verification_required: Explain old behavior vs new behavior
State whether protection was added in bot_core.py only or also at model_bridge source
Run py_compile on each changed Python file
State whether restart is required for the fix to take effect
